text:
MRI NECK WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST
Post-Treatment Surveillance

CLINICAL INDICATION: 52-year-old female with HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the left base of tongue (T2N1M0, stage III per AJCC 8th edition). Treated with definitive concurrent cisplatin chemotherapy and intensity-modulated radiation therapy (70 Gy in 35 fractions). Completed chemoradiotherapy approximately 12 weeks prior. PET-CT performed on the same date demonstrates complete metabolic response. MRI obtained for anatomic characterization of post-treatment changes and evaluation for residual disease.

COMPARISON: MRI neck with contrast performed prior to initiation of treatment demonstrating 2.8 x 2.1 cm left base of tongue primary mass and 2.5 x 2.0 cm left level IIA lymph node with central necrosis.

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the neck with and without intravenous gadolinium contrast (gadopentetate dimeglumine 0.1 mmol/kg IV). Sequences obtained include axial and coronal T1, T2, and fat-saturated T1 post-contrast, as well as sagittal T2 and DWI with ADC maps. Dedicated head and neck protocol. Motion degradation: minimal. Diagnostic quality: good.

FINDINGS:

Primary Site - Left Base of Tongue:
The previously identified 2.8 x 2.1 cm heterogeneously enhancing mass at the left base of tongue is no longer visualized. In its place, there is diffuse mucosal thickening along the left base of tongue and extending to the left vallecula, without formation of a discrete mass or nodule. The mucosal surface is somewhat irregular, consistent with expected post-radiation mucosal changes. On T2-weighted sequences, there is mild increased signal in the treated region, in keeping with post-treatment edema and fibrosis. No focal area of restricted diffusion on DWI to suggest residual viable tumor. No abnormal nodular or mass-like enhancement is identified at the primary site. The tongue base musculature demonstrates mild diffuse T2 hyperintensity and patchy enhancement diffusely, consistent with radiation-related inflammatory and edematous changes rather than infiltrating tumor. Pre-epiglottic space: preserved, no mass or infiltration. Epiglottis: normal morphology. The right base of tongue appears unremarkable.

Cervical Lymph Nodes:
Left Level IIA: The previously enlarged left level IIA lymph node measuring 2.5 x 2.0 cm with central necrosis is markedly decreased in size. A small residual soft tissue structure measuring approximately 8 mm in short-axis diameter is noted at this location. This structure demonstrates homogeneous T1 isointense and T2 mildly hyperintense signal without internal necrosis or rim-enhancing morphology. Peripheral enhancement is minimal and smooth, without the irregular or heterogeneous pattern seen on the pre-treatment scan. The previously noted central necrosis is completely resolved. Findings are most consistent with treatment response and residual fibrotic nodal tissue rather than viable nodal metastasis, particularly in the context of the concordant PET-CT showing no FDG avidity at this site.

Left Levels IB, IIB, III, IV, V: No pathologically enlarged or morphologically suspicious lymph nodes identified. No necrotic or rim-enhancing nodes.

Right Neck (Levels IB, IIA, IIB, III, IV, V): No significant adenopathy. Several small sub-centimeter nodes are noted at levels II-III bilaterally, all within normal size limits and without suspicious morphologic features.

Retropharyngeal and Parapharyngeal Spaces:
No retropharyngeal or parapharyngeal lymphadenopathy. No mass. Parapharyngeal fat planes appear preserved bilaterally, though mildly edematous on the left consistent with radiation field effects.

Larynx and Hypopharynx:
Laryngeal framework intact. True and false vocal cords appear symmetric with no mucosal lesion identified. Piriform sinuses patent bilaterally without mucosal irregularity. Subglottis unremarkable. No laryngeal or subglottic mass.

Salivary Glands:
Parotid glands: Bilateral mild volume loss compared to estimated pre-treatment baseline, with slightly heterogeneous parenchymal signal on T2. Fatty replacement noted more prominently on the left. Findings are consistent with post-irradiation salivary gland changes. No discrete masses or ductal dilation.
Submandibular glands: Mildly decreased in volume bilaterally with mild T2 heterogeneity. No obstructing calculus or mass identified. Changes are compatible with radiation-related parenchymal injury.
Sublingual glands: Unremarkable.

Vascular Structures:
Bilateral carotid arteries are patent with flow voids maintained on standard sequences. No encasement or vessel wall irregularity to suggest tumor involvement. No significant atherosclerotic changes at the level of the carotid bifurcations on this examination.

Mandible and Skull Base:
Mandibular cortex intact bilaterally. No cortical breakthrough, periosteal reaction, or marrow signal abnormality to suggest osteoradionecrosis or bony invasion. Skull base: Normal signal and morphology. No perineural tumor spread along cranial nerve pathways. Foramen ovale and hypoglossal canals are symmetric and unremarkable.

Soft Tissues of the Neck:
Diffuse mild T2 signal hyperintensity in the subcutaneous soft tissues and superficial musculature of the bilateral anterior neck within the radiation field, consistent with post-treatment edema. No organized fluid collection, abscess, or fistula tract identified. Skin and subcutaneous tissues demonstrate mild thickening but no deep space soft tissue infection.

Thyroid Gland:
Thyroid gland demonstrates slightly heterogeneous signal. No discrete nodules or masses above the resolution threshold of this examination. Mild volume reduction compared to expected normal, potentially related to radiation field effects.

Cervical Spine:
Visualized cervical vertebral bodies and disc spaces are unremarkable. No significant spondylosis at the levels included in the field of view. Spinal canal appears adequate without cord signal abnormality at the visualized levels.

IMPRESSION:

1. No discrete residual mass or abnormal nodular enhancement identified at the left base of tongue primary site. The region shows post-treatment mucosal thickening and diffuse radiation-related edema without features concerning for residual viable tumor. This is in concordance with the same-day PET-CT demonstrating no FDG-avid disease at the primary site.

2. The previously enlarged left level IIA lymph node with central necrosis has undergone dramatic interval reduction, now represented only by an 8 mm homogeneous soft tissue structure without necrosis or suspicious enhancement. Appearances are consistent with a robust nodal response with residual fibrotic tissue. No FDG avidity on correlative PET-CT.

3. No new or suspicious cervical lymphadenopathy in either neck.

4. Post-radiation changes in the bilateral salivary glands, soft tissues, and mucosa of the oropharynx are noted and expected given the treatment history.

5. No bony changes to suggest osteoradionecrosis of the mandible.

6. No evidence of residual or recurrent disease at this 12-week post-treatment evaluation.
